servinfosub.title: Cranking Circuit
servinfosub.ptxt: The cranking circuit consists of the battery, starting motor, starting motor control relay, keyless start control module, engine switch, CPP No.1 switch (M/T model) or transmission range sensor (CVT model), and related electrical wiring.

servinfosub.title: Starting Motor Circuit
servinfosub.ptxt: In the circuit shown in Cranking System Circuit Diagram , the coil of the magnetic switch in starting motor is energized when the engine starts. The resulting movement of the plunger and pinion drive lever causes the pinion to engage with the engine flywheel (M/T model) or drive plate (CVT model), while closing the contacts of the magnetic switch. This causes the starting motor to rotate to crank the engine.
When the engine starts, the pinion overrunning clutch protects the armature from excessive speed until the switch is opened, at which time the return spring causes the pinion to disengage.
